description:
Goal
Compute a root/executable-scoped runtime demand closure from sealed semantic facts, without recovering obligations from types.

Problem being fixed
compiler2 already has hard-won semantic facts: activation inputs, value types, callsite summaries, target summaries, reachable executables, and return delivery needs. The missing piece is a single backward demand propagation pass that uses those facts and preserves exact demand.

Code seams
- src/compiler2/jobs/root.rs: seal_semantic_closure and executable_callsite_needs currently determine which executable facts belong to the semantic closure.
- src/compiler2/jobs/semantic.rs: analyze_activation currently discovers exact call targets and then derives runtime_callable_values locally.
- LoweredBody producers/consumers are the natural graph for backward demand propagation.

Algorithm
1. Build a producer/consumer index for each LoweredBody: value -> producing step/tail/input, value -> structural children for tuple/list/map/struct/lambda captures, callsite -> argument values and target summaries.
2. Seed demand from executable boundary needs: entry return demand, external/provider/runtime/dbg/send/spawn boundaries, dispatch destinations, opaque closure calls, and explicit escaping values.
3. Seed resolved callable demand from CallSiteSummary.targets[*].surface_inputs for closure-call targets that are actually invoked.
4. Propagate demand backward through existing producer structure: tuples, lists, maps, structs, lambda/function refs, captures, activation inputs, and call arguments.
5. Propagate across calls using callee input_demands and return_demand. This is demand flowing over the existing executable graph, not a new semantic discovery mechanism.
6. Iterate to a stable normalized demand plan for each executable in the SemanticClosure.

Invariants
- ActivationKey.input remains semantic and complete.
- Runtime input_demands are a derived layout plan and may omit semantic inputs.
- Exact resolved callable surfaces come from CallTargetSummary.surface_inputs.
- Opaque and escape are represented explicitly; do not encode them as "callable input position" booleans.

Telemetry
Emit fz.compiler2.runtime_demand.defined after demand settles. Include counts that tests can assert without dump strings: executables, demanded_inputs, omitted_inputs, demanded_values, tuple_field_demands, direct_callable_materializations, first_class_callable_materializations, opaque_callable_demands, escaped_callable_demands.

Removal target
Delete runtime_callable_boundary_values, note_callable_arg_uses, ClosureBoundaryUse::CallableArg, closure_value_needs_runtime_boundary, and semantic-time runtime callable materialization decisions.

Checks
Add tests that observe telemetry or demand facts for:
- unused callable input: semantic input exists, runtime input demand is Ignore.
- direct lambda call: resolved callable demand records exactly one surface.
- escaped callable: escape demand produces first-class materialization.
- opaque callable call: opaque demand is explicit and does not invent resolved surfaces.
title: Compiler2 runtime demand: compute demand closure from semantic evidence
